2605.30144.title: AgentSchool: An LLM-Powered Multi-Agent Simulation for Education
2605.30144.paper_summary: Despite the rapid deployment of LLMs into classrooms, validating educational AI remains uniquely intractable: interventions act on developing learners whose cognitive and social trajectories are irreversibly shaped, while real-world trials are slow, ethically constrained, and institutionally locked. LLM-based educational simulators have emerged as a potential remedy, but many still collapse learning into persona-conditioned role-play and, when optimized only to reproduce existing classrooms, can structurally penalize the institutional novelty that pedagogical reform requires. In this work, we introduce AgentSchool, an LLM-driven multi-agent simulator that models learning as state transition rather than prompted behavior. AgentSchool couples cognitively growable student agents -- equipped with weighted subject knowledge graphs, thinking-workflow pools, and explicit misconceptions -- with adaptive teacher agents that plan, scaffold, and reflect along the Zone of Proximal Development, embedded in a configurable scenery generator that situates instruction within both formal and informal learning fields, and a multi-scale simulator that decouples interaction scale, temporal granularity, and simulation duration. Experiments show that structured student agents produce more differentiated mastery and misconception traces than a baseline simulator, while teacher-agent comparisons show backbone-dependent patterns consistent with ZPD-informed adaptation. Further, AgentSchool generates plausible traces of peripheral participation, clique formation, aggressor-induced cohesion, and opinion-leader emergence consistent with classroom social theories. Beyond its role as an educational research instrument, AgentSchool frames education as a socially meaningful testbed for long-horizon memory, multi-agent coordination, and future institutional reasoning under organizational pressure.
2605.30144.paper_summary_zh: 儘管大型語言模型（LLMs）迅速進入教室，但驗證教育人工智慧仍然獨具挑戰性：干預措施作用於正在發展的學習者，其認知和社交軌跡是不可逆轉的，而現實世界的試驗則進展緩慢，受到倫理約束，並被機構鎖定。基於LLM的教育模擬器已經出現，作為潛在的解決方案，但許多仍然將學習簡化為基於角色的扮演，並且當僅優化以重現現有教室時，可能會在結構上懲罰教育改革所需的機構創新。在這項工作中，我們介紹了AgentSchool，一個由LLM驅動的多代理模擬器，將學習建模為狀態轉換，而不是提示行為。AgentSchool將可以認知成長的學生代理（配備加權的學科知識圖、思維流程池和明確的誤解）與適應性教師代理結合在一起，這些教師代理在最近發展區域內計劃、搭建支架和反思，並嵌入在一個可配置的場景生成器中，該生成器將教學置於正式和非正式學習領域之中，並在一個多尺度模擬器中解耦互動尺度、時間粒度和模擬持續時間。實驗顯示，結構化的學生代理產生了比基線模擬器更具差異化的掌握和誤解痕跡，而教師代理的比較顯示出與ZPD知情適應一致的骨幹依賴模式。此外，AgentSchool生成了與教室社會理論一致的邊緣參與、團體形成、施暴者引發的凝聚力和意見領袖出現的合理痕跡。超越其作為教育研究工具的角色，AgentSchool將教育框架設置為一個具有社會意義的測試床，以進行長期記憶、多代理協調和在組織壓力下的未來機構推理。
2605.30144.author: Yulei Ye et.al.
2605.30144.authors: Yulei Ye, Wenhao Li, Zhong Wen, Yunshu Huang, Yichen Hu, Zifan Wei, Yige Wang, Xinyu Xie, Haoxuan Yang, Yanjun Huang, Ruijia Li, Hong Qian, Yu Song, Bo Jiang, Bingdong Li, Lijun Li, Bo Zhang, Pinlong Cai, Xingcheng Xu, Shuangye Chen, Xia Hu, Liang He, Aimin Zhou, Jingjing Qu, Jing Shao, Xiangfeng Wang
